structured_payload_candidate.title: Valve Authority for HVAC Coil Applications
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Valve authority in the range of 30 to 50% is adequate for HVAC coil applications with 2:1 branch-to-riser pressure drop ratios. Higher Δt coils require less valve authority. Authorities less than 30% should be avoided.

evidence_text:
1.4 2024 ASHRAE Handbook—HV AC Systems and Equipment  (SI)
• System integration with other building systems
• Other HV AC goals• Security and environmental health and safety criteria• Building envelope• Project timeline (design, equipment delivery, commissioning,
training, and construction)
• Basis of design• HV AC system analysis and selection matrix• System narratives• Budget costs (first cost, operating cost, and energy cost)• Final recommendation(s)
A brief outline of each of the final selections should be provided
along with overall budget and/or cost per square metre. In addition,
HV AC systems deemed inappropriate should be noted as havingbeen considered but not found applicable to meet the owner’s pri-mary HVAC goal.
The report should include an HV AC system selection matrix that
identifies the one or two suggested HV AC system selections (pri -
mary and secondary, when applicable), system constraints, and
other constraints and considerations.
judge_reason: Evidence mismatch: the provided evidence covers general HVAC system selection outline, not valve authority values. Actual source likely elsewhere.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Coil Rating Parameters for Dehumidifying Coils
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Dehumidifying coils are rated within parameters including entering air dry-bulb 18 to 38°C, air face velocity 1 to 4 m/s, and entering chilled-water temperature 1.5 to 18°C.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low-Temperature Heating System Supply Water Temperatures
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Low-temperature heating systems are generally designed with supply water temperatures from 80 to 115°C and temperature drops from 10 to 55 K.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Coil Rating Entering Air Wet-Bulb Temperature
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Entering air wet-bulb temperature for coil ratings is 15 to 30°C if air is not dehumidified in the application.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Dehumidifying Coil Sensible Heat Ratio Range
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The air-side ratio of sensible to total heat removed by dehumidifying coils varies from about 0.6 to 1.0 (sensible heat is 60 to 100% of total).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Standard Rating Entering Air Conditions
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Standard ratings at 26.7°C db and 19.5°C wb are representative of entering air conditions for many comfort operations.
